title: Thick as Thieves won't be getting any more content updates: 'To be frank, this is not the outcome that any of us want'
summary: For $5, it did seem too good to be true.
body: OtherSide Entertainment's stealth game Thick As Thieves launched in May of this year, but felt like it should have been in early access . It only had two maps, and though its campaign changed the objectives as it bounced you back and forth between them in either co-op or solo play, it felt thin. Frustrations like the specifics of how its time-limit system worked and the absence of options like rebindable keys at launch didn't help. While the first post-launch content update did give us rebindable keys at least, it wasn't enough to fully turn around the fortunes of Thick as Thieves. Which was a problem, because the other game OtherSide was working on was cancelled, and now it's had two rounds of layoffs leaving fewer than 10 employees remaining. "Working in the gaming industry is at times delightful and at other times heartbreaking", says a message posted by game director Jeff Hickman , and ain't that the truth. Hickman goes on to confirm that " only a very small team remains " and therefore Thick as Thieves won't be receiving any further content updates. "To be frank," he writes, "this is not the outcome that any of us want, but we're facing the reality that ongoing development of…

title: Star Wars Zero Company reveals cast, including fan favorite actors from Clone Wars animated series
summary: The crowd is going nuts, they want their Dee Bradley Baker, they're chanting Matt Lanter's name: "Lan-ter, Lan-ter, Lan-ter!"
body: Ahead of a showing at Comic-Con in San Diego, StarWars.com has revealed the main cast of Star Wars Zero Company, the squad tactics game by ex-Firaxis XCOM vets. The topline for big Star Wars freaks: Dee Bradley Baker and Matt Lanter will be reprising their long-running roles as clone troopers and Anakin Skywalker respectively. (Image credit: Disney) Baker always reminded me of the actors in Elder Scrolls games (like the inimitable Wes Johnson ) in the way he elevated the high-volume job of voicing every clone trooper for like, a decade . It's something you could see becoming rote or workmanlike, but across multiple shows (most notably Dave Filoni's The Clone Wars), Baker put some zip into named clones and one-line mooks alike. His main role in Zero Company will be Trick, one of the game's RPG-like story companions. Lanter similarly defined Anakin Skywalker in the animated Filoni-verse. When I got to try Zero Company hands-on back in February, I clocked Baker as Trick immediately. The Summer Game Fest reveal that Anakin Skywalker will appear in Zero Company had me wondering if the Chosen One would get a similar treatment. Elsewhere in the cast, Vic Michaelis of the popular Dropout …

title: A new expansion and the ability to be a crow wizard has me enjoying a 10-year-old action RPG for the first time
summary: Grim Dawn's Fangs of Asterkarn expansion pulled me in despite its old age.
body: I'm not even that far into my first ever playthrough of Grim Dawn and I can already see why I've heard people praise this game for the last 10 years. It's old, sure, but it's a surprisingly tight action RPG that despite its age hasn't been left to rust. The developers at Crate Entertainment have kept Grim Dawn from feeling like an action RPG that came out a decade ago. The quality-of-life additions in its final expansion, Fangs of Asterkarn , do a lot of the heavy-lifting, but even visually the game doesn't look that far removed from an older World of Warcraft zone. I've been playing quite a lot of Path of Exile 2 lately and was a little scared that Grim Dawn would be far too archaic for me. I think a major reason that hasn't been the case is that Fangs of Asterkarn gave it full WASD movement support, a control scheme I have a hard time letting go of in these types of games. It works so flawlessly in Grim Dawn that I would've assumed it was there from the start if I hadn't read the patch notes. My initial class choice inadvertently gave me a taste of some of the OG experience. I went with an occultist, thinking it would be fun to play the closest thing to a spell-casting warlock t…

title: Atari signs 10-game deal with Universal for movies based on Pong, Breakout, Centipede, and yes, really, Pong
summary: Get ready for 90 minutes of Liam Neeson punching holes in brick walls, or... something.
body: A Deadline report says Atari, in partnership with management and production company Entertainment 360, has signed a deal that gives Universal Pictures the right to turn 10 of Atari's best-known games into movies. "For more than five decades, Atari has created games and worlds that have remained part of popular culture long after their original release," Atari chairman and CEO Wade Rosen told the site. "We are excited to work with Universal and Entertainment 360 to bring the spirit of our iconic brand and games to a new medium." The games in question are undeniably Atari classics, although not necessarily obvious fodder for film adaptations: Asteroids Adventure Berzerk Breakout Centipede Crystal Castles Millipede Missile Command Pong Yes, Pong Yars’ Revenge The intent, according to the report, is to turn the properties into "big-scale action-adventure fare." "Matt Reilly and I grew up playing Atari 2600 games and have remained fans ever since," said Guymon Casady of Entertainment 360, a producer on the first film to come out of the deal. "The best Atari games dropped you into a world and let your imagination do the rest. "[Screenplay writers Carl Hampe and Matt Reilly] saw an oppor…

title: Amazon and Microsoft pivot cloud gaming strategies to target different players

title: Ubisoft chief says Sony's decision to kill game discs isn't going to be a big deal
summary: Yves Guillemot reckons the lack of physical discs might even be a good thing.
body: Sony's decision to stop producing game discs in 2028 has caused a real uproar. Some PlayStation owners see a dire future as they become dependent on digital services and lose the ability to trade old games in for new ones, while at least one analyst says we'll all get used to it quickly enough anyway. Ubisoft CEO Yves Guillemot has now weighed into the debate, and he's clearly in the "no big deal" camp. Guillemot was asked during today's quarterly investors call if Sony's decision might create "a demand headwind," because of the loss of the secondary market for PlayStation games. "What we saw on the PC is that it helped to grow the market," Guillemot said, presumably a reference to the explosive growth of Steam that drove a stake through the heart of physical PC game releases. "There's also some pressure for the future on the cost of machines, and being able to be only digital will help to have more accessible machines, I would say. There are pluses and minuses, but we think it will not disturb the industry too much." The impact of Steam on the evolution of PC gaming is obvious and undeniable, and while you might argue that it's not entirely a good thing (I certainly would), PC ga…

title: Crisol studio lays off all employees less than 6 months after launch, warns that full closure is a 'feasible possibility'
summary: Initial reports that Vermila Studios had shut down were premature, but it's definitely in trouble.
body: Less than six months after the release of the survival horror shooter Crisol: Theater of Idols , developer Vermila Studios appears to be in trouble. The studio has not officially closed, as one former employee had claimed, but CEO David Carrasco confirmed that employees have been let go and that a full shutdown "is a feasible possibility." Vermila's reported closure was initially shared on X by game designer Raúl Pernía Alejandro, who said (via auto-translation) that "Vermila Studios has initiated a redundancy procedure that has ultimately led to the studio's closure and the departure of the entire team." Several hours after that message was posted, however, it was deleted and replaced with one stating that while the "entire team" has left, "for now the company remains active." Alejandro acknowledged the mistake in a subsequent apology. (Image credit: Rauwuu_ (Twitter)) Still, it was clear that layoffs had taken place, as numerous employees on X and LinkedIn, some of them in senior positions and with long tenures and Vermila, reported that they'd been let go on July 22. Carrasco subsequently confirmed the cuts in a statement provided to Game Developer , saying that Vermila had not…

title: MSI celebrates 40 year anniversary by unveiling a SEVEN GRAND LAPTOP
summary: You'd need a dragon's hoard to afford this.
body: My favourite hardware news stories lately have all followed a similar line of logic: 'Why? Well, why not?' But when it comes to the laptop MSI just unveiled as part of its 40th anniversary, I can't move past 'But why though?' The sticking point for me is the Titan 18 HX Dragon Edition's ludicrous $7,000 price tag . Sure, you're getting an Nvidia RTX 5090 GPU with a pretty face-melting 24 GB of GDDR7, plus an Intel Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor . And, sure, some versions of the RTX 5090 will set you back a couple grand all on their own, but not usually the slimmer laptop kind. Anyway, 7,000 smackers is still a lot of money for the power. I hate to say it but the oodles of SSD storage and the whopping 96 GB of DDR5 might have something to do with it (thanks, memory crisis). Hey, at least if you buy directly from MSI, you also get a free code for Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight. This very pricey gaming laptop also enjoys an 18-inch MiniLED screen, which features a maximum resolution of 3840×2400. The refresh rate is a not too shabby 120 Hz, but at this price point I was kind of expecting something fast enough to make my eyes roll back in my head. Joking aside, I'm struggli…

title: Where to find tank lattes in Forza Horizon 6
summary: Smash 20 cute tank lattes in Ine.
body: Tanks for the Coffee is one of the new challenges in Forza Horizon 6 's Series 3 Autumn series, requiring you to smash 20 tank lattes for a frankly measly reward. While it gives you the hint to head to Ine, that's easier said than done if you're not familiar with the map. Plus, everyone's currently driving around there trying to break tank lattes, so it's a bit of a fight to get what you need. Forza Horizon 6 tank latte locations Xbox Xbox Xbox You'll find tank lattes in the town of Ine on the north end of the Ito region on the east coast. You'll see lattes spawn on the side of the road and along the dock—including the driveways next to houses, which can be harder to spot. There are tons of spawns around here, but a good place to start is on the main road through the town, where multiple spawn around the Minka house and the Aftermarket Car location. All you have to do to smash them is drive through them at speed. Unfortunately, other players can break these tank lattes, which prevents you from scoring a point, like the kind of cursed enemy kill camping you see in MMOs. If this happens, just keep on searching and double back later if you still need more. For completing the Tanks fo…

title: Google slapped with $1 billion fine for violating EU's antitrust legislation
summary: Continued non-compliance could result in further penalties of 'up to 5% of [Google's] total worldwide turnover'.
body: The European Commission announced today it will fine a total of Google €890 million—or about $1 billion—for two violations of the EU's Digital Markets Act. The Search giant has 60 days to either address the violations or risk further penalty payments of "up to 5% of its total worldwide turnover" in the future. The Commission explained it is fining Google for "self-preferencing its own services on Google Search, and for putting in place restrictions on businesses to direct consumers to alternative, often cheaper, purchase channels on Google Play (steering)." These two violations have resulted in a fine of €460 million and a fine of €430 million respectively. Google has already begun work to redress these DMA violations by beginning to test "changes to how it presents its own services on Google Search for free services such as shopping, hotels and flights." The company will also review how it "presents shopping ads and content related services, such as sports." Google will apparently also make similar adjustments to AI Overviews and AI Mode. The European Commission says it will "continue its dialogue with Google," and "monitor the implementation of these solutions which constitute s…

title: Amazon walks back comments about taking 007 off of IO, but does say 'anything's possible' when it comes to buying the studio outright
summary: Not quite what I wanted to hear.
body: Amazon really confounded us all last month when, following the successful launch of 007 First Light, Amazon Gaming general manager Jeff Gattis mused that sequels would be "done by MGM and, theoretically, by Amazon Game Studios." Given that so many people—barring one uncontroversial and universally beloved PC Gamer writer—rather liked IOI's Bond game, it was incomprehensible to suggest yanking it away from the studio. Well, I guess Amazon itself has realised what a boneheaded move that would be, because in a recent chat with GI.biz , Gattis clarified that—insofar as the developer of the next 007 game is concerned—nothing is set in stone. "We did not confirm anything about who's publishing or developing the next Bond sequel… we haven't even discussed it really yet. The game's awesome. That's great. I think it's realistic to think that there would be a sequel." Then he states the obvious: "Given the success of this one, I think you probably want IOI developing it." Taken in tandem with an earlier comment, that "Technically, Amazon does own the rights to the game, but we're going to do what's right by the game, we're not trying to bring everything into Amazon," it does at least sound …

title: Early '90s animation tools were so primitive that Doom's lead artist says he pretty much did the whole thing with 'the undo key'
summary: The UnDoom key, if you will.
body: Human ingenuity is a powerful—sometimes terrifying—thing. Throughout history, no matter how basic the tools available to us, we have always found a way to build the weird obsessions that appear in our minds. You know, like Stonehenge, or the Pyramids, or Doom (1993). No, really. In a recent chat with Jesse Petrilla on YouTube , id co-founder and Doom lead artist Adrian Carmack revealed that, when it came time to put together the classic FPS' animations, the incredibly complex and advanced animation tool he used to do it was… the undo key. "As an artist, oftentimes you end up making your own tools," said Carmack. "You know, there was no animation tool, so I started using the undo key to create animation—the undo key in Deluxe Paint, so I'd go back and forth." If you're unclear as to how that works, remember that animations in original Doom weren't really animations as we think of them today, with models seamlessly transitioning through countless subtle movements to create smooth motion. Doom sprites, from your enemies to your weapons, were all very frame-y indeed. Working with relatively few frames meant Carmack could create and capture animation loops by cycling through frames in …

title: Lick your way to victory with the Mouth Pad, a Bluetooth device that lets you control your PC with just your tongue
summary: An early prototype looked like 'a lollipop with a bunch of sensors.'
body: PC gaming isn't always the most inclusive space. Besides the high price of everything from components to games, standard gaming peripherals such as a keyboard and mouse can exclude lots of folks with limited mobility. An expanding array of assistive devices is attempting to bridge that gap. Augmental describes itself as a company specialising in 'Skinware,' "body-coupled interfaces that let people control computers, communicate, and interact with AI without relying on their hands." Its latest project is the Mouth Pad, a custom-fit mouthpiece that allows users to control PCs, tablets, and phones with just their tongue. The device is now available to purchase in the US and, yes, you can absolutely game with it. The Mouth Pad looks like the top half of a retainer, with flexible circuits encased inside custom 3D printed dental resin. Mouth-based physical interfaces are nothing new in the assistive tech space , and mouth-operated pens are already used by gamers with physical disabilities. Rather than bashing your teeth against a physical joystick, Mouth Pad users can instead either run their tongue over the trackpad covering the roof of their mouth, or engage a head tracking mode in or…

title: Star Wars Zero Company is a 30-40 hour linear game with no New Game+, and I'm totally OK with this
summary: I'll actually be able to finish it. Hurray!
body: As someone who frequently finds themselves embroiled in gargantuan, branching RPGs and spends far too much time in live service romps, I really appreciate a game I don't need to dedicate my life to—a nice palate cleanser that's one and done. And it looks like Star Wars Zero Company falls into that category. In a recent Discord Q&A , developer Bit Reactor tackled the subject of game length, and while I certainly wouldn't call it short, it does sound pretty manageable. "Depending on your difficulty level and experience with the strategy genre, we estimate about 30-40 hours for the average player to complete the main story campaign," said lead producer Caydence Funk. We also know it's going to be a linear deal , with no branching paths or romances between operatives. So there are not a whole lot of reasons to dive back in once you've already concluded your Clone Wars misadventure. There's no New Game+ mode, either, though Funk notes that "players will unlock certain customisation options on subsequent playthroughs". I doubt I'll feel compelled to play again just for some extra customisation options, and honestly without an NG+ mode I feel like Bit Reactor should just give us everythi…

title: Hideo Kojima's worked out exactly how he wants to die: Kicking the popcorn bucket
summary: Gotta admire a man who knows what he likes.
body: Hideo Kojima sure has been thinking about death a lot lately. Though he's a pretty spritely 62 years old, the Metal Gear and Death Stranding creator has made our pages recently for things like: talking about deciding to make Physint after a brush with death in 2020 , making a game where you get old and die , and how he has a USB stick full of ideas that he will one day leave to his staff that is "kind of like a will". Well, here's one more for the pile: Kojima knows how he wants to die. In a post on X (translated by Genki_JPN ), Kojima said that—when the time comes—he wants to kick the popcorn bucket. No, really, he wants to die in a cinema: "I'm someone who's always thought when I die, I want to die in a movie theater." Like a man who's spent most of his life directing videogames down to their last detail, Kojima's got the whole thing mapped out in his mind. "I like the time before the movie starts the best," he says. "Enter the movie theater, the lights turn off, the commercials and trailers play, then just before the movie starts is when I want to pass away." Probably not a fun time for the guy who comes in to clean up the seats and finds him, but there's nothing to be done abo…

title: Indie game contest tasks devs with fitting their entire game on to a 1.44 MB floppy disk
summary: Creativity out of some pretty strict confines.
body: 1,474,560 bytes may sound like a lot for those unfamiliar with the realities of modern data storage, but it amounts to a single 3.5-inch floppy disk. The hey-day of such a relic of physical media has long since passed, but floppy disks obviously still hold a certain allure for some judging by a certain Korean game dev contest. Anyone can participate in the contest from Korean indie game site 2P Game Arcade, and the rules are very simple: devs just need to build a game that totals no more than 1,474,560 bytes (or 1.44 MB) after decompression (via Techspot ). Though there are no restrictions on which engine devs may use, the work itself must be an original creation made after the contest announcement date on May 26. The teeny-tiny game must also be playable as a standalone executable (browser-based games are not eligible). Devs have until midnight on September 4 to turn in their work. There are prizes to be won, though a far cry from the $80,000 the EVE Online devs recently awarded to its own mod contest winners . The machine translation of the 2P Game Arcade contest page describes these prizes as only 'a small souvenir,' with first place taking home 720,000 won (about $490). The ju…

title: Star Wars Zero Company won't have official mod support, confirms Bit Reactor, but it has 'a lot of respect for the modding community'
summary: But there's still hope.
body: Star Wars Zero Company developer Bit Reactor is made up of former XCOM veterans , a series which has a pretty lively modding scene, especially XCOM 2, which supports modding with official development tools. It's even had its fair share of Star Wars mods over the years that completely transport it to a galaxy far, far away. Mod support is one of the reasons XCOM is still holding ground on players' hard drives all these years later. Despite the success this has brought to Firaxis' series, Bit Reactor has confirmed in a new Discord Q&A that Zero Company won't have official mod capabilities. "We have a lot of respect for the modding community," says lead producer Caydence Funk, "but there is no official mod support planned for Star Wars Zero Company." Of course, this could change post-launch if it proves a hit, but the devs have made it clear we're not getting official modding tools at release. It's a shame given all the creative XCOM Star Wars mods willing Zero Company into existence years ago. Plus, it's also one of the most requested features in the game's Discord server, so it's not like there's not an audience. My guess is it's down to simple development priorities. In a month-ol…

title: 'Far beyond anything we had predicted': Framework says its supplier's memory prices are 'more than double' its previous shipment
summary: The company says it "goes far beyond anything we’re able to absorb without placing our ability to operate at real financial risk."
body: Over the last few months, Framework has been doing a pretty good job of keeping RAM prices for its modular laptops/mainboards/desktops from climbing too high as a result of the global memory shortage , though storage hasn't fared quite so well . And now, RAM unfortunately isn't faring so well, either. The company explains in its monthly memory and storage pricing update that its memory module supplier has raised its prices substantially. And unfortunately it's to the extent that Framework isn't going to be able to eat the extra cost. "We recently received a cost update from our LPCAMM2 supplier that goes far beyond anything we had predicted and anything we’re able to absorb without placing our ability to operate at real financial risk. Rather than the low-to-mid double digit percentage increase we had forecast from Q2 to Q3 on LPCAMM2 costs, we received prices that are more than double that of the prior inventory we had brought in." Framework's explanation for what it's having to change given this is a little confusing, but essentially it looks like all remaining 64 GB module stock the company has will cover already-existing laptop and mainboard pre-orders using these modules, but…

summary: Start by focusing on earnings

title: The challenges of developing the colony sim, from Dungeon Keeper to Dwarf Fortress and beyond
summary: PC Gamer interviews the genre's founding fathers.
body: From the archives: This feature was originally published in PC Gamer #305 (UK, June 2017) . It appears below as originally written, with only minor changes in formatting and presentation. No gaming genre can generate stories quite like the colony sim. Almost entirely unique to PC gaming, colony sims task players with building and sustaining a habitat for a group of AI characters, which could be anything from dwarfs to demons. Although the player controls how the colony is shaped, constructing rooms or buildings and filling them with useful objects, they do not control the inhabitants directly. Instead, the colonists interact with their surroundings and other NPCs via AI systems that simulate their needs and desires. Through these desires and the player’s ability (or inability) to meet them, colony sims produce incredible, incidental plotlines; tales of dwarves who delve too greedily and too deep, unleashing hordes of monsters into their fortress, of bitter rivalries between demonic creatures that tear apart the plans of an evil dungeon master, of prisoners who explode into riot because the warden forgot to build a shower block. It’s the autonomous personalities of these AI individ…

title: Steam update makes gifting much easier, adds wishlist organization features
summary: Aunts and uncles rejoice: Sending gifts on Steam has been simplified.
body: Buying someone else a Steam game got a lot easier today. You no longer have to be Steam friends with the person to whom you're sending a gift, and don't even need a Steam account. You can also select gifts directly from a shared wishlist. Here are today's Steam gifting changes , in short: It's now possible to gift a game without having a Steam account. (Previously, guest checkout could only be used for digital gift card and hardware purchases) You also don't need to know the recipient's Steam account, and can gift games and digital gift cards with just an email address (even if it isn't the address associated with their Steam account) You can now gift games across regions. You need to know the recipient's Steam account for this, as the price you pay will be the price of the game in their region You can now select and purchase gifts directly from a shared wishlist Previously, sending a gift on Steam meant asking the recipient for their Steam account name and friending them, which dampens the surprise a bit. My only wish now is for a simple way to stuff a Steam game into a physical card for birthdays and holidays. Steam retired physical gift cards recently, citing fraud. (With these…

title: Blizzard investigating World of Warcraft cheating scandal after game master 'death-touched' bosses for friends without realizing it was being recorded
summary: One of the players then went on a Twitch stream to spill the beans.
body: A cheating scandal has erupted in the World of Warcraft community after a Blizzard developer was caught using special game master (GM) abilities to kill two bosses for some friends. The GM seemingly didn't realize that one of the players was recording combat logs of the whole thing. Late on Tuesday, Reddit user Acrobatic_Pianist688 posted evidence of the GM's meddling on the WoW subreddit. Their post links to a combat log that shows a non-player ability called "Area Death (TEST)" being used to instantly kill a boss with nearly 90% of its health left. Not long after, a commenter on the thread found the GM using a separate insta-kill ability on a boss earlier in the run , too. Surprisingly, one of the players from the accused group went onto a Twitch stream to talk about what happened . When streamer Yumytv asked them to explain, they said they were just short of finishing the Mythic+ dungeon in time to get a seasonal title when they called for help from their GM friend: "I go to my GM friend and I'm like bro please can you do me a solid? This thing has 20 million [health] bro, can you please just one-shot it?" The player went on to say that they don't care if Blizzard disqualifies …

title: Indie game gets last minute Steam delay while Valve reviews its 'dozens of untextured polygons depicting ass and boobs'
summary: Future? No Thanks! is the latest game to get caught up in Steam's ambiguous content guidelines.
body: Earlier this month, indie political satirist game dev Molleindustria—pseudonym of Paolo Pedercini, who created games like Democratic Socialism Simulator and New York Times Simulator —announced that he was moving up the release of his latest game. Despite having previously promised to launch the game on the same day as GTA 6 "in order to humiliate Rockstar in the open-world genre and deliver the killing blow to the Western game industry," Future? No Thanks!, would instead launch today, on July 22. Now that the day has arrived, Molleindustria's launch plans have changed once again. The developer announced a last minute delay for Future? No Thanks! on Bluesky, saying that the game has been the subject of an unexpectedly lengthy Steam review process due to the presence of nudity during one of the game's sequences. Some sad news regarding today’s release: — @molleindustria.org ( @molleindustria.org.bsky.social ) 2026-07-22T19:08:49.374Z Using a sarcastic tone characteristic of Pedercini's previous work, Molleindustria said that its launch timeline estimates "were foolish to disregard the only authority on the matter: Steam, which has kept our game under review for more than three weeks…

title: StarCraft 2 is included in Blizzard's BlizzCon digital goodies, which is more evidence a new game will be announced soon, right?
summary: Is it just me or is Blizzard gearing up to announce the rumored StarCraft shooter?
body: Not to fully transform into the Pepe Silvia meme pointing at a wall of "evidence" but I'm convinced Blizzard is gearing up to announce something StarCraft-related at BlizzCon this year. The first reliable clue came from Bloomberg reporter Jason Schreier's 2024 book on Blizzard's history where he said the studio was "incubating" a StarCraft shooter ( not that one ) under the helm of former Far Cry producer Dan Hay. A couple of years passed with no news on that StarCraft project. But in April of this year, Blizzard was looking to hire a lead designer of innovation for an "AAA open-world shooter" using Unreal Engine—a game engine that it's never used before. Blizzard game designer Jeff Hamilton said on Bluesky that the person who got the job would have a "fairly unique design role" as part of the team. While that was the last direct evidence for a new StarCraft game, Blizzard recently surprised-dropped a massive patch for StarCraft 2 , which feels like the kind of thing you'd do to remind people you still care about the series. The update wasn't just a bunch of bug fixes and minor tweaks either. Blizzard said the focus was to spice it up with meta-changing tweaks to early- and mid-ga…

title: Microsoft is bringing original Xbox exclusives to PC for the first time, starting with four 'classic games'
summary: Xbox Backward Compatibility on PC, as it's known, is now live.
body: As it continues to try smoothing the waters churned up by the recent layoff of thousands of people , Xbox has followed up last week's big Fallout infodump with the launch of a new " Backward Compatibility on PC " program that will bring old-time Xbox games, previously exclusive to the platform, to PC and compatible handhelds for the first time. The program is launching with four "classic original XBOX games," as Microsoft described them. I'm not sure I'd use that characterization myself, but to be fair I haven't played any of these games (although I did enjoy the original Crimson Skies), and PC Gaming Show maestro Jake Tucker informs me that "Conker was the shit." I am not convinced, but anyway, here's the starting lineup: BLiNX: The Time Sweeper Conker: Live and Reloaded Crimson Skies: High Road to Revenge Fuzion Frenzy All four games are now available for purchase on PC and are also offered through all Game Pass plans, and if you already own a license for them on console that will carry over to your PC as well. As well as being playable on new hardware, the PC-compatible versions will offer new features including customizable graphics options, and language and audio settings. (I…

title: Star Wars Zero Company's story 'is a linear one from beginning to end', Bit Reactor says, so don't expect branching paths or romance options
summary: "We are excited about pushing the genre forward with a more cinematic approach."
body: With Star Wars Zero Company , developer Bit Reactor aims to create a tactics RPG with characters and narrative at the forefront . It's got proper, big-budget cutscenes, you can talk to your buddies, and you can customise and upgrade your squad. However, the developers have gone on record in a new Discord Q&A to say, no, don't expect to have control over how the story unfolds. Earlier this year, our own Ted Litchfield spoke to narrative director Aaron Contreras , who explained that while Bit Reactor wants its cast to have real friction, Star Wars Zero Company is "not a personal fantasy game." Those "player-centric romance" options that seem to pop up in so many games today won't appear in Zero Company. This was reaffirmed in today's Q&A, where the developer plainly states, "No, there aren’t any romance systems or branching paths" in Zero Company. "While we are excited about pushing the genre forward with a more cinematic approach, this is still a turn-based tactics game at its heart where adept skill and strategy are needed to overcome tense combat challenges." "That said, there are story moments that can alter based on who you have in your squad, but the overall story is a linear …

title: Acer and Gigabyte will launch RTX Spark laptops too, but not before Microsoft and Co.
summary: The first batch not including Acer and Gigabyte will launch this fall.
body: Nvidia's Arm-based processor for laptops was unveiled after much waiting at Computex back in June. RTX Spark is Nvidia's first proper go at a consumer chip, combining CPU and GPU, and while it is of course targeted at AI workloads, it's also targeted at gaming. Now, Nvidia has confirmed which will be the first brands of RTX Spark laptop to come to market this fall and which will follow those. According to Nvidia , "RTX Spark systems will be available this fall from Asus, Dell Technologies, HP, Lenovo, Microsoft Surface and MSI, with models from Acer and Gigabyte to follow." The initial few aren't a big surprise given these are the exact companies that were announced as partners when RTX Spark was revealed. Of note is Acer and Gigabyte following the initial six, though. As Chinese news site the Commercial Times points out (via VideoCardz ), MSI and Asus will be the first Taiwanese companies to launch Spark lappies. But given it was the Surface Laptop Ultra that RTX Spark was most commonly shown running on at Computex, it'll be surprising if that's not the first one to market. That being said, I'm looking forward to RTX Spark laptops other than the Surface. Back when it was announce…
